marketFocus: US-Iran tensions escalate: Trump sets 15-day deadline, weighs limited military strike
Fed's Miran dials back cut outlook to 100bps (from 150bps), citing firmer labor data and goods inflation
Private credit concerns: Blue Owl halts redemptions, sparking sell-off in financials
Japanese inflation cools below BoJ target (1.5% vs 2.1%), providing policy space
Today: US GDP Q4, Core PCE, Global Flash PMIs, CAD Retail Sales, UK Retail Sales

USD.name: USD
USD.summary: USD strengthens on safe-haven flows amid US-Iran tensions and Fed's Miran hawkish pivot. Jobless claims fell to 206k (below 225k exp), signaling labor market resilience. Trade deficit widened to $70.3B (exp $55.5B), but USD supported by geopolitical premium and Fed repricing.
drivers: Trump sets 15-day Iran deadline, weighs limited military strike
Fed's Miran now sees 100bps cuts (prev 150bps), citing firmer labor and goods inflation
Initial jobless claims fell to 206k vs exp 225k
Trade deficit widened to $70.3B vs exp $55.5B
Today: GDP Q4, Core PCE, Flash PMIs

EUR.name: EUR
EUR.summary: EUR under pressure, failing to hold 1.1800 amid weaker Consumer Confidence and geopolitical risk aversion. ECB President Lagarde signals commitment to finishing term but door not closed on early departure. Focus shifts to Flash PMIs today.
drivers: EUR/USD failed to sustain 1.1800, now testing 1.1747
Consumer Confidence missed expectations
ECB President Lagarde signals commitment but door not closed on early exit
ECB's Demarco: Inflation below 2% could need cut
Today: Flash Manufacturing PMI, Flash Services PMI

GBP.name: GBP
GBP.summary: GBP retreats to sub-1.3500, testing 200 DMA amid dovish BoE Mann comments and weak growth environment. Mann stated core inflation 'not quite as good as hoped' and unemployment rise is 'very much of a concern', raising speculation of March/April cut.
drivers: GBP/USD testing 200 DMA at 1.3439
BoE Mann: Core inflation not quite as good as hoped
BoE Mann: Unemployment rate rise is very much of a concern
Tepid UK growth environment weighs on sentiment
Today: Retail Sales, Flash Manufacturing PMI, Flash Services PMI

JPY.name: JPY
JPY.summary: JPY weakens as USD/JPY lingers near 155.00 after Japanese inflation cooled below BoJ target (1.5% vs 2.1%). Core CPI matched 2% target (2-year low), providing BoJ additional policy space. Despite BoJ March hike odds at 60%, cooling inflation reduces urgency.
drivers: USD/JPY hovering around 155.00, near 100 DMA
Japanese CPI fell to 1.5% vs prev 2.1%, below BoJ 2% target
Core CPI at 2% (2-year low), matched expectations
Cooling inflation provides BoJ additional policy space
BoJ March hike odds at 60% despite softer inflation

AUD.name: AUD
AUD.summary: AUD initially bid post-jobs data (unemployment 3.5% vs exp 3.6%), but succumbed to USD strength and risk-off sentiment. Mixed employment report showed headline miss but full-time jobs growth. China markets remain closed until Tuesday, limiting AUD upside.
drivers: Unemployment rate 3.5% vs exp 3.6% (supportive)
Employment change slightly missed expectations
Full-time jobs growth sole driver of employment increase
Succumbed to USD strength and risk-off sentiment
China markets closed until Tuesday (Lunar New Year)

CAD.name: CAD
CAD.summary: CAD choppy and pressured amid reports Trump admin could scrap USMCA in favor of bilateral deals with Canada and Mexico. Canadian officials cite 'very low' expectation for full USMCA renewal. Trump reportedly trying to weaken Canada economically to force policy changes.
drivers: Trump admin reportedly indicates USMCA could be scrapped
Canadian officials: Expectation for full USMCA renewal very low
Trump trying to weaken Canada economically to force policy changes
CAD and MXN choppy on USMCA uncertainty
Today: Retail Sales m/m, Core Retail Sales m/m

CHF.name: CHF
CHF.summary: CHF mixed, benefiting from safe-haven flows amid US-Iran tensions but pressured by EUR weakness. Swiss Industrial Production fell -0.70% Y/Y (prev 2.40%), signaling economic slowdown. SNB policy outlook unchanged.
drivers: Safe-haven flows support CHF amid geopolitical tensions
EUR weakness weighs on CHF crosses
Swiss Industrial Production fell -0.70% Y/Y vs prev 2.40%
SNB policy outlook unchanged
Risk-off sentiment provides mild support

NZD.name: NZD
NZD.summary: NZD underperformed post-RBNZ dovish pause, with Westpac cutting fixed home loan rates across 3-5 year terms. RBNZ Governor Breman stated policy will adapt based on new information, not predetermined path. NZD breached prior day's trough amid risk-off sentiment.
drivers: Westpac cuts NZ fixed home loan rates across 3-5 year terms
RBNZ Governor Breman: Policy will adapt, not predetermined path
RBNZ dovish pause weighs on NZD
NZD breached prior day's trough amid risk-off
China markets closed until Tuesday limits upside

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes saw two-way trade with modest gains, but Fed's Miran hawkish pivot (100bps cuts vs 150bps) limits upside. Geopolitical tensions and private credit concerns (Blue Owl redemption halt) provide mild support. Focus shifts to Core PCE and GDP data today.
drivers: Fed's Miran now sees 100bps cuts (prev 150bps), hawkish repricing
Initial jobless claims fell to 206k, signaling labor resilience
Private credit concerns: Blue Owl halts redemptions
Geopolitical tensions provide mild safe-haven bid
Today: Core PCE, GDP Q4 data

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bunds extended rebound with little to derail momentum ahead of German PPI data. ECB President Lagarde signals commitment to finishing term but door not closed on early departure. Weak Consumer Confidence and geopolitical tensions support bonds.
drivers: Bunds extended recent rebound
ECB President Lagarde signals commitment but door not closed
Weak Consumer Confidence supports bonds
Geopolitical tensions provide safe-haven bid
Today: German PPI, Flash PMIs

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: JGB futures gained following softer-than-expected Japanese CPI data. Headline CPI slipped below BoJ 2% target for first time in almost 4 years (1.5% vs 2.1%), while core CPI matched 2% goal (2-year low). Cooling inflation provides BoJ additional policy space despite March hike odds at 60%.
drivers: Japanese CPI fell to 1.5% vs prev 2.1%, below BoJ 2% target
Core CPI at 2% (2-year low), matched expectations
Cooling inflation provides BoJ additional policy space
BoJ March hike odds at 60% despite softer inflation
JGB futures gained on softer inflation data

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts softer and marginally underperforming, possibly a move away from UK assets (GBP and FTSE 100 lagging) as tensions between UK and US increase re. Diego Garcia. BoE's Mann dovish tilt raises speculation of March/April cut.
drivers: Gilts underperforming, off lows but towards trough
BoE Mann dovish tilt: Core inflation not quite as good as hoped
Tepid UK growth environment weighs on sentiment
UK-US tensions increase (Diego Garcia, Iran strike bases)
Today: UK Retail Sales, Flash PMIs
